title: Launch Vehicle Sizing Tool
description: Build a complete launch vehicle sizing tool that takes a payload requirement and target orbit, and produces a preliminary vehicle design — number of stages, engine selection, propellant mass, structural mass, and performance prediction.
whyItMatters: Vehicle sizing is the core of launch vehicle preliminary design. It integrates every discipline — propulsion, structures, astrodynamics, and systems engineering — into a single trade study tool. This is the kind of tool that SpaceX uses to evaluate Starship variants.

steps.title: Define the architecture
steps.description: Build a parametric vehicle model: number of stages (1-3), engine type per stage, structural mass fraction per stage, and propellant type. Allow modular configuration.
steps.tips: Use object-oriented design — each stage is an object with engines, tanks, and structure.

steps.title: Implement the sizing loop
steps.description: Starting from payload mass and orbit delta-v, work backwards through each stage using the Tsiolkovsky equation. Include gravity and drag losses. Iterate until convergence.
steps.tips: The sizing loop converges in 5-10 iterations. Start with an initial guess of total mass = 100x payload.

steps.title: Add engine database
steps.description: Include real engine options (Merlin, Raptor, RS-25, BE-4, RL-10) with full performance data. Let the tool select optimal engine count per stage.
steps.tips: Engine count affects both thrust-to-weight ratio and structural mass. More engines = more mass but more redundancy.

steps.title: Optimise staging
steps.description: Implement a staging optimisation that finds the optimal velocity split between stages for minimum total mass. Compare 1, 2, and 3-stage designs.
steps.tips: The optimal staging split is rarely 50/50. First stage typically does 35-45% of total delta-v.

steps.title: Generate the report
steps.description: Output a complete vehicle summary: stage masses, propellant loads, engine counts, total height, performance predictions, and comparison with existing vehicles.
steps.tips: Generate a vehicle schematic diagram showing relative sizes of stages and components.

deliverables: Parametric trade studies for different payload classes
Comparison with Falcon 9, Starship, and Vulcan
Written report on design methodology and results

rubric.description: Tool produces vehicle masses within 15% of real vehicles when given the same requirements
Clear visualisation of how payload, orbit, and engine choice affect vehicle size
Modular, extensible code that can easily add new engines or mission types
Demonstrates understanding of how subsystem choices interact to drive vehicle design
